# But CLI: Incident & Workaround History

Tracked during [PR #1: Compile `but` CLI to WASI](1/INDEX.md).
Retrospective: [RETRO.md](RETRO.md).

## Failures

| ID | Category | Command | What Happened | Root Cause | Workaround | Impact | Source |
|----|----------|---------|---------------|------------|------------|--------|--------|
| F1 | commit | `but commit` (stacked branch) | Committing to branches stacked on 0-commit parents produces "object with id 000...000 not found" | Null parent commit reference when stack base has no commits | Use standalone branches anchored on `feat/wasi`, not stacked on empty branches | Blocked initial commit workflow; forced workaround for all early sub-PRs | [s01 MEMORY.md](1/s01/feat/wasi-feature-flags/MEMORY.md) |
| F2 | hunk-lock | `but rub`, `but stage`, `but commit` | Plan files (INDEX.md, MEMORY.md) absorbed into s04 commit (`2729b64`). Attempts to reassign via `but rub`/`but stage`/`but commit` produced empty commits on s05 branch | Race condition: hunk lock mechanism treats files created by same parent commit (`ddf6dfc`) as belonging together. Multi-agent staging to branches in same stack triggers this | None effective. s05 left with two empty commits (`d5bea01`, `eb0277c`). Files correct on disk but in wrong branch. Fix deferred to implementation phase (amend will include all content) | s05 branch corrupted; required coordinator intervention and workaround plan | [s05 MEMORY.md](1/s01.s05/feat/wasi-gate-process/MEMORY.md) |
| F3 | commit | `but commit` | Null commit IDs returned silently — no error message, no non-zero exit code. Claude retried 5+ times per session with different flags, never succeeding | Unknown; `but commit` returns null IDs under certain workspace states without surfacing the underlying error | Wasted sessions retrying; eventually abandoned approach entirely | 4+ sessions affected, cascading branch corruption, 2 "not_achieved" outcomes | Claude Code insights report |
| F4 | sync | (background) | TOML file edit literally reverted by GitButler on its own — changes made by Claude were undone without warning | GitButler background sync overwrites in-progress edits when it detects working tree changes that conflict with its internal state | None; must detect and re-apply edits. No way to pause background sync | Lost work, undermined trust in file system state during agent operations | Claude Code insights report |
| F5 | branch-mgmt | `but branch` | `but` does not support branch rename operations | Feature not implemented in CLI | Delete branch and recreate with correct name | Minor; adds friction when naming conventions evolve mid-project | Claude Code insights report |
| F6 | amend | `but amend` (in a stack) | Amend operations shift commit IDs throughout the entire stack. Any previously-read commit ID becomes invalid after a single amend. Subsequent operations silently target wrong commits | Commit ID instability: `but amend` mutates the commit graph, invalidating all IDs above the amended commit in the stack | Created `but-amend-all` (T8) that re-reads commit ID after each amend and detects infinite loops from cross-stack hunk locks | Cascading corruption requiring constant cleanup; every amend workflow must be written defensively with re-read loops | Claude Code insights report; [but-amend-all](../../scripts/bin/but-amend-all) |

## Bespoke Workaround Tools

All tools live in `scripts/bin/` with logic in `scripts/but_functions.sh`. See [SKILLS.md](SKILLS.md) for usage docs.

| ID | Tool | Why Created | What It Does | Missing `but` Capability | Source |
|----|------|-------------|--------------|--------------------------|--------|
| T1 | `but-apply-pattern` | Need to bulk-activate branches matching a pattern; `but apply` takes one branch at a time | Runs `but branch list --json`, filters with jq for branches matching regex, calls `but apply` on each match | Batch apply by pattern (`but apply --pattern <regex>`) | [scripts/bin/but-apply-pattern](../../scripts/bin/but-apply-pattern) |
| T2 | `but-unapply-pattern` | Need to bulk-deactivate branches matching a pattern; `but unapply` takes one branch at a time | Filters `appliedStacks[].heads[]` from `but branch list --json` for matching names, calls `but unapply` on each | Batch unapply by pattern (`but unapply --pattern <regex>`) | [scripts/bin/but-unapply-pattern](../../scripts/bin/but-unapply-pattern) |
| T3 | `but-stage-all` | Need to stage multiple file/hunk IDs at once; `but stage` takes one ID at a time | Iterates over provided IDs, calls `but stage <id> <branch>` for each. Reports success/failure count | Batch staging (`but stage <id1>,<id2>,... <branch>`) | [scripts/bin/but-stage-all](../../scripts/bin/but-stage-all) |
| T4 | `but-changes` | `but status --json` output is verbose and hard to scan; need quick filtered views | Pipes `but status --json` through jq with 4 modes: `--unassigned`, `--branch <name>`, `--all`, `--summary` | Filtered/formatted change views; human-readable output mode for status | [scripts/bin/but-changes](../../scripts/bin/but-changes) |
| T5 | `but-branch-ids` | Need compact tabular view of branches with cliIds, commit counts, status; `but status --json` buries this in deep JSON | Pipes `but status --json` through jq, formats with `column` for tabular output. Optional regex filter | Human-readable branch listing with metadata | [scripts/bin/but-branch-ids](../../scripts/bin/but-branch-ids) |
| T6 | `but-branch-commits` | Need to see commits on a specific branch with details; requires deep jq traversal of `but status --json` | Extracts commits from `but status --json` for a named branch; shows cliId, message, author, file count | Per-branch commit detail view (`but show <branch> --format table`) | [scripts/bin/but-branch-commits](../../scripts/bin/but-branch-commits) |
| T7 | `but-diff-files` | Need to see working tree file changes quickly; `but` has no equivalent of `git diff --name-only` | Runs `git diff HEAD --name-only` or `git diff HEAD --stat`. **Note: falls back to `git` because `but` lacks this** | Working tree diff listing (`but diff --name-only`) | [scripts/bin/but-diff-files](../../scripts/bin/but-diff-files) |
| T8 | `but-amend-all` | `but amend` shifts commit IDs in a stack (F6); amending multiple hunks requires re-reading commit ID after each. Also need to detect infinite loops from cross-stack hunk locks | Iterates hunks one-at-a-time. After each `but amend`, re-reads commit ID via `but status`. Detects when same hunk reappears 3 times (cross-stack hunk lock) and skips. 50-iteration safety cap | Stable commit IDs across mutations; bulk amend; hunk lock detection/reporting | [scripts/bin/but-amend-all](../../scripts/bin/but-amend-all) |
| T9 | `but-overview` | Need a human-friendly dashboard of workspace state; `but status --json` is raw JSON | Formats unassigned changes + branch commits with conflict markers from `but status --json`. Optional `--json` passthrough | Human-readable status summary/dashboard as default output | [scripts/bin/but-overview](../../scripts/bin/but-overview) |
| T10 | `but-setup-branches` | Need to create entire branch topologies from a JSON config with proper dependency ordering; `but branch new` creates one at a time with no topological ordering | Reads `agents.json`, performs topological sort of dependencies via jq, creates branches in order with `--anchor` flags. Supports `--dry-run` and `--clean`. **Note: `--clean` uses `git branch -D` because `but` lacks branch deletion by pattern** | Topology-aware batch branch creation; branch deletion by pattern | [scripts/bin/but-setup-branches](../../scripts/bin/but-setup-branches) |
